index.heading: Wireframes
index.subtitle: Interactive wireframes for stakeholder review
footer.name: Project Name
footer.disclaimer: These wireframes are for internal review only.
example.title: Example Page
example.subtitle: Component showcase
example.heroText: This wireframe demonstrates every available template component. Use it as a reference when building new pages.
example.feature1Desc: Toggle the Scope button in the top bar to see MVP annotations, notes, and issue tracker links on every section.
example.feature2Desc: Each page has a review status badge: WIP, Review, With client, or Approved. Set per-page in data.ts.
example.feature3Desc: All copy lives in strings/en.json for easy editing and future localisation. No hardcoded text in components.
example.contactText: A post-MVP contact form placeholder. The scope overlay marks this as post-MVP with an orange bar.
content.title: Content Page
content.subtitle: Informational
content.introText: This is a generic long-form content page pattern. Use it for about pages, policy pages, guides, or any page with structured body copy and optional sidebar links.
content.bodyP1: Body sections use the full container width with consistent margins. Each section can be wrapped in a WireframeSection for scope annotations.
content.bodyP2: Use SectionLabel for uppercase kicker text above headings. Use ImagePlaceholder for media slots. Use StatCard for key figures.
content.bodyP3: The scope overlay system works identically on content pages — toggle Scope in the top bar to see annotations.
sitemap.label: Information Architecture
sitemap.heading: Site Map
feature-status.label: Feature inventory
feature-status.heading: Feature Status
feature-status.description: Every section across every wireframe, with MVP / post-MVP scope and rationale. Derived from scope annotations.
feature-status.empty: No sections match the current filter.
accessibility.label: Accessibility
accessibility.heading: Accessibility statement
accessibility.conformance: We aim to meet WCAG 2.2 Level AA across every page. Where we fall short, we list known gaps below and welcome feedback.
accessibility.feedbackText: If something is not accessible, please let us know so we can fix it.
